corrected_assertion: loadSupersessions() now reads BOTH sidecar extensions, preferring the new .amendments.jsonl and falling back to .supersessions.jsonl. The function name is preserved (loadSupersessions) because the JSON field names and verb forms (superseded_at, supersedes) are retained as standard English — only the noun ('amendment record') and the file extension ('.amendments.jsonl') change. New implementation pseudocode: const candidates = [memoryFilePath + '.amendments.jsonl', memoryFilePath + '.supersessions.jsonl']; const sidecar = candidates.find(p => existsSync(p)); if (!sidecar) return [];. This is load-bearing for backwards compatibility — the operator's live ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/*.supersessions.jsonl corpus continues to work without migration.
source: git commit d5c9f5a 'feat(EBR): rename accretive supersession → Evidence-Bound Retrieval (EBR)' 2026-05-27
